data.title: Dutch Bros. (BROS) Stock Soars 30% on Blowout Earnings
data.description:
The stock of Dutch Bros. (BROS) is up 30% after the retail coffee chain reported blowout financial results for the fourth quarter of 2024.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The Oregon-based company delivered another in a series of impressive prints, announcing earnings per share (EPS) of $0.07 on revenue of $343 million. That topped earnings of $0.02 and sales of $318 million that was expected on Wall Street.

At the same time, Dutch Bros guided for full-year 2025 revenue of $1.55 billion to $1.57 billion and same store sales growth of 4%. The guidance also exceeded analysts’ consensus expectations, sending the stock up sharply higher in today’s trading (Feb. 13).

Red Hot Stock

BROS stock has now risen 208% in the last 12 months. The company has a unique business model in that it only operates drive-through coffee locations. A going concern since 1992, the company primarily operates in the western U.S., mostly in its home state of Oregon, as well as neighboring California.

However, Dutch Bros. is expanding, having recently opened a new location in Florida. Analysts say the continued expansion should help Dutch Bros. earnings in coming quarters. Dutch Bros. is one of several coffee chains to report strong financial results, with Starbucks (SBUX) and Restaurant Brands International (QSR), parent of Canada’s Tim Hortons, also reporting solid prints.

Is BROS Stock a Buy?

The stock of Dutch Bros. has a consensus Strong Buy rating among nine Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on nine Buy recommendations issued in the last three months. The average BROS price target of $72.11 implies 13.80% downside from current levels. However, these ratings are likely to change after the company’s financial results.

Read more analyst ratings on BROS stock
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: “No $400M Armored Tesla Contract” says Elon Musk as State Dept. Drops Order from Procurement List
data.description:
The U.S. State Department is not planning to award Tesla (TLSA) a $400 million contract to purchase armored vehicles. At least that’s what appears to be the case following the latest twist in a bizarre story about procurement and Tesla armored vehicles that has raised questions about Elon Musk’s role within the new administration.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The State Department was planning to buy $400 million of “Armored Tesla” later this year, according to its 2025 procurement forecast, first detailed by reports in Drop Site News and the New York Times.

Procurement List Drops Tesla Reference

But suggestions of a conflict of interest due to Musk’s proximity to the White House and his role at the newly-minted Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) may have prompted a change of heart. 

An earlier version of the document, which can still be found, listed “Tesla Armored Electric Vehicles.” But an updated version has amended this to just “Armored Electric Vehicles,” while leaving in a separate line still referencing BMW AG (DE:BMW), whose armored X5 and X7 SUVs the State Department also wants to buy.

It’s unclear whether this actually means there never was a plan to purchase Tesla armored vehicles, or if the State Department has ditched its intention to make those vehicles Tesla, or if it still plans to go ahead with the Tesla purchase anyway while removing the reference to Tesla in the procurement list. 

Responding to developments, Musk replied, “I’m pretty sure Tesla isn’t getting $400M. No one mentioned it to me, at least.” 

It’s worth noting that the original State Department procurement form was from before the Trump administration took over.  

Interestingly, the original document mentioning Tesla listed the planned procurement under NAICS Code 311999, which Time notes is an industry code for miscellaneous food manufacturing. The updated version features NAICS Code 561613 for “Armored Car Services.” 

Is TSLA Stock a Buy? 

On TipRanks, TSLA stock has a Hold consensus rating based on 13 Buys, 12 Holds, and 10 Sell ratings. Also, the average Tesla price target of $340.50 implies 1% upside potential from current levels. In the past year, Tesla stock has gained over 80%.

See more TSLA analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: Alphabet (NASDAQ:GOOGL) Signs AI Deal with Poland after Lifting Weapons Ban
data.description:
Alphabet (GOOGL) has signed a memorandum of understanding with Poland to speed up adoption of artificial intelligence in the country. Amid mounting fears in Warsaw that the U.S. might force Ukraine to do a peace deal with Poland’s neighbour Russia, the deal happens to come just days after Google lifted a ban on using AI for weapons and surveillance, although the announcement does not appear to make explicit reference to use of AI in defense.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

GOOGL Attends Paris AI Action Summit

It also comes as Google CEO Sundar Pichai met European leaders at the AI Action Summit in Paris this week, where the U.S. and the UK refused to sign a declaration on “inclusive and sustainable” artificial intelligence.

The summit was marked by an insistence on fostering AI innovation, with U.S. Vice President Vance cajoling European Union leaders in particular into being more positive and less “risk averse” about the technology. The deal between Poland and GOOGL maybe suggests the message is cutting through.

“We are excited to announce a new memorandum of understanding to use AI to drive economic growth and help transform sectors, including cybersecurity, health, and energy,” Pichai told reporters, according to Reuters.

Poland Wants AI to Help Defend Itself

In November, Poland said it would invest roughly $240 million in AI development to enhance its economic competitiveness and strengthen national security due to growing hybrid threats from Russia. The plan involves creating an Artificial Intelligence Fund and an advisory council to coordinate investments, which will include an AI Factory.

According to Forbes, Poland’s deputy prime minister and digital affairs minister, Krzysztof Gawkowski, said, “There’s hardly a day now when critical infrastructure isn’t targeted by Russian services.”

And last week Polish President Andrzej Duda that his country plans to spend 4.7% of GDP on defense this year. Poland already spends 4.1% of its GDP on defense, more than any other NATO member. Lithuania intends to raise its spending on defense to between 5% and 6% of GDP starting in 2026. 

GOOGL Ditched Peaceful AI Principles 

On February 4th, a blog post from senior GOOGL executives signalled the company was lifting its self-imposed ban on harnessing AI for weapons or surveillance. 

James Manyika, Google senior vice president for research, technology, and society, and Demis Hassabis, CEO of Google DeepMind, said the evolving landscape of AI and geopolitical struggles meant its principles had to change. 

So, a note added to a 2018 webpage did away with language promising not to pursue “technologies that cause or are likely to cause overall harm,” and “weapons or other technologies whose principal purpose or implementation is to cause or directly facilitate injury to people”. The amended AI principles also removed references to banning the use of “technologies that gather or use information for surveillance violating internationally accepted norms,” and “technologies whose purpose contravenes widely accepted principles of international law and human rights.” 

The principles had first been published in 2018 in response to criticism over Google working on a U.S. military drone contract, which it chose not to renew. 

But the update does away with peaceful principles, stating that GOOGL will implement “appropriate human oversight, due diligence, and feedback mechanisms to align with user goals, social responsibility, and widely accepted principles of international law and human rights.” 

Is GOOGL a Good Stock to Buy? 

With 27 Buys and 10 Holds, Wall Street views GOOGL as a Moderate Buy. The average GOOGL price target stands at $215.85, suggesting a potential 17.5% upside from current levels.

See more GOOGL analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: Elon Musk’s Meme Coin Maneuver Sends Solana Tokens Tumbling
data.description:
In a situation that could only come from the world of crypto and high-profile personalities, Solana (SOL-USD) meme coins took a nosedive this Wednesday. The reason? Tech titan Elon Musk decided to drop his cheeky moniker, “Harry Bōlz.” This caused an immediate impact on the value of two meme coins named after his erstwhile X account pseudonym. The market caps of these tokens, which had soared to $25 million and $8.5 million respectively just a day earlier, plummeted following Musk’s decision to revert to his original X handle.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

From Media Stunts to Market Plunges

It all started as a media stunt in April 2023 when Musk adopted the “Harry Bōlz” name, which prompted the launch of the related meme coins. However, the fun didn’t last long. As reported by André Beganski, the coin named HarryBolz crashed to nearly zero, while its counterpart, HARRYBOLZ, dropped by 54% in just 24 hours. Musk’s antics, reminiscent of a “The Simpsons” prank call, should caution investors about the volatile nature of meme coins that thrive on public attention and celebrity endorsements.

Remember When KEKIUS Surged 3000%

This isn’t the first time Musk’s online behavior has rocked the crypto boat. Similar fluctuations occurred in early January with the Solana meme coin Kekius Maximus (KEKIUS), which surged 3,000% after Musk mentioned his video game character. Like the others, KEKIUS’s spike was short-lived, crashing back down once Musk stopped promoting it.

Musk’s name changes and the ensuing market reactions exemplify how closely digital currencies can be tied to media visibility and celebrity movements. This should serve as a reminder of the speculative and sensitive nature of meme coin investments.

Investors should monitor well-estsblished cryptocurrencies on TipRanks. Click on the image below to find out more.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Morgan Stanley (NYSE:MS) Closing $3B X Debt in Big Win for Lenders and Elon Musk
data.description:
Wall Street banks led by Morgan Stanley (MS) are close to offloading another $3 billion in debt relating to Elon Musk’s purchase of Twitter. 

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Coming after lenders successfully sold $5.5 billion of loans that had been stuck on their books for over two years it marks another big win for the banks and for Musk. 

Both Bloomberg and the Financial Times reported that Morgan Stanley was aiming to price the secured loans paying 9.5% in interest with no discount after they secured sufficient demand for the paper.  

It marks the third sale in a month for the Twitter buyout financing and would take the total to $9.5 billion. MS first sold $1 billion of debt at around 90-95 cents on the dollar while the $5.5 billion tranche was sold at 97 cents on the dollar. 

That only leaves about $3 billion left on their balance sheets to offload. 

Big Win for MS and other Lenders 

Seven banks, including MS, Bank of America (BAC) and Barclays (BCS), put up around $13 billion in financing to help Musk secure Twitter, now known as X, for $44 billion in 2022. The other banks involved in the deal were Mizuho (MFG), MUFG (MUFG), Société Générale (SCGLY) and BNP Paribas (BNPQY).

While they had hoped to provide temporary financing for Musk, the loans have been left lingering on their balance sheets as his attempt to back out of the transaction rattled would-be investors. The Federal Reserve’s decision to raise interest rates quickly also dampened enthusiasm for the transaction among investors.  

However, Musk’s own personal stock – and therefore the ability of the banks to sell the debt – has been rising alongside the election of President Donald Trump. It’s thought that Musk’s proximity to the President and role within the White House will help rather than hinder his business interests. 

X’s stake in Musk’s artificial intelligence startup xAi has also helped sentiment towards the debt. 

Is MS a Good Stock to Buy? 

Overall, Wall Street has a Moderate Buy consensus rating on MS stock, based on five Buys and 10 Holds. The average MS price target of $143.50 implies almost 5% upside from current levels.

See more MS analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: Lucky Strike Owner British American Tobacco (NYSE:BTI) Gets Toasted on Regulatory Headwinds and Canada Lawsuit Costs
data.description:
British American Tobacco (BTI) stock plunged on Thursday amid a dismal outlook and soaring costs from a long-running lawsuit in Canada.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The Pall Mall and Lucky Strike owner said it expects “significant regulatory and fiscal headwinds” in Bangladesh and Australia to impact performance in traditional cigarette sales in 2025.  

Despite it booking a £6.2billion ($7.74 billion) hit from outstanding litigation in Canada, the company reported annual profit from operations of £2.7 billion, up from a loss of £15.8 billion last year.  

However, shares in London (GB:BATS) fell over 9% as revenues missed forecasts for the year, falling 5.2%, driven by the sale of its businesses in Russia and Belarus in September 2023. Moreover, cigarette volumes were down by 5% on an organic basis, a steeper decline than the broader industry’s 2% contraction last year. 

Guidance was muted with the company saying it expects adjusted profit to grow between 4% and 6% next year, alongside revenue growth of 3-5% in 2026 as it shifts focus to smokeless products.

While management would like to tell a story of smokeless products replacing declining cigarette sales, BTI reported new product revenue growth of just 2.5%, albeit on an organic basis the 8.9% looks healthier.

Canada Lawsuit Costs Weigh 

The litigation costs in Canada relate to a decade-old landmark court ruling in Quebec that found the company and two other cigarette makers had failed to sufficiently warn customers of the health risks associated with smoking. BTI, Philip Morris (PM) and Japan Tobacco (JAPAF) were ordered to pay CA$15.6 billion in damages  

Commenting on the results today, CEO Tadeu Marroco  said, “We are committed to Building a Smokeless World and becoming a predominantly Smokeless business by 2035.” 

Smokeless products such as Vuse and Velo, now account for 17.5% of the group’s revenue, after it added 3.6 million customers to this category last year.

Is British American Tobacco a Good Buy? 

Turning to the London-listed shares, BATS is rated a Moderate Buy based on four Buys, three Holds and one Sell. The average BATS price target of 3,335.71p implies around 8% upside. BATS trades on the NYSE under the ticker $BTI.

See more BATS analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: Ben & Jerry’s Maker Unilever to List Ice Cream Business in Amsterdam, London and New York
data.description:
Unilever (UL) will list its demerged ice cream business in Amsterdam, London and New York, the company said today as it posted underwhelming full-year results. 

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

The ice cream business, which includes brands such as Magnum and Ben & Jerry’s, generated turnover of €8.3 billion ($8.62 billion) in 2024. The business will be incorporated in the Netherlands and will continue to be headquartered in Amsterdam.  

Its primary listing will be in Amsterdam and will be chaired by Jean-Francois van Boxmeer, currently chair of Vodafone Group (VOD) and non-executive director of Heineken (HEINY), having been chief executive of the Dutch brewing giant for 15 years.

It marks something of a coup for Amsterdam after Unilever combined its dual Anglo-Dutch structure to a single London-based entity. Shell (SHEL) also ended its dual share structure in 2022, dropping its listing in Amsterdam in favour of London.

Unilever Sales Growth Falters 

The news came as Unilever reported sales rose 4% in the Fiscal fourth quarter as price rises remained subdued despite “strong performances” from its core “power brands” Dove, Comfort and Vaseline. 

Unilever (GB:ULVR) shares in London declined over 6% as the company also reported underlying sales growth of 4.2% in the year to December, a little beneath expectations for growth of 4.3%, with volumes up 2.9% and pricing softer at +1.3%. 

Overall turnover was marginally down on last year, while net profits were down over 10% to €6.4 billion. 

CEO Hein Schumacher added that growth in the first half of 2025 would be soft despite big spending on brand marketing at 15.5%, the highest in over a decade. The company raised its dividend by 6.1% and announced a new €1.5 billion share buyback. 

Is Unilever Stock a Good Buy?  

Overall, Wall Street rates ULVR a Moderate Buy, based on 11 Wall Street analysts offering 12-month price targets for Unilever in the last 3 months. The average ULVR price target is 4,881.96p, implying around 10% upside from current levels. The stock is traded in New York under the ticker $UL.

See more ULVR analyst ratings
data.author: Neil Wilson

data.title: Ripple Case Braces for Monumental Shift as SEC Reevaluates Crypto Rules
data.description:
The U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) and Binance have approached a federal judge in Washington, D.C., seeking a temporary halt to their ongoing enforcement actions. This strategic pause is part of the SEC’s effort to reevalute the murky waters of digital asset regulations through its newly established crypto task force. The outcome of this pause could significantly influence the ongoing Ripple (XRP-USD) litigation. This reflects a potential shift in the SEC’s regulatory approach toward cryptocurrencies.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

Ripple Could Benefit from the SEC’s New Strategy

The ongoing legal battle involving Ripple demonstrates the broader uncertainty surrounding the SEC’s jurisdiction over cryptocurrencies. John Reed Stark, a former SEC official, views this development as a possible turning point in the SEC’s enforcement strategy. Stark suggests that this could lead to favorable resolutions for cases like Ripple’s, or perhaps even a complete reassessment of pending litigation within the sector.

Stark Issues a Grave Warning

Despite the possible softening of the SEC’s stance, Stark remains vocal about the risks inherent in the crypto industry, particularly its association with fraud and criminal activities. He argues that diminishing the SEC’s crypto enforcement could unleash widespread regulatory challenges, likening the potential fallout to a contagion that could affect the entire global market.

After a recent legal win against the SEC, Ripple’s XRP has seen a significant uptick in market confidence, now trading at $2.4457.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: Stock Market News Today, 2/13/25 – Futures Down as Investors Await Inflation Data
data.description:
U.S. futures edged lower on Thursday morning, as investors looked forward to another key inflation data and several corporate earnings releases. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA), and the S&P 500 (SPX) were down 0.12%, 0.12%, and 0.21%, respectively, at 3:39 a.m. EST, February 13.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

On Wednesday, all three major indices witnessed a dull performance. The S&P 500 and the Dow Jones declined 0.2% and 0.5%, respectively, while the Nasdaq Composite (NDAQ) rose less than 0.1%.

Hotter-than-expected January Consumer Price Index (CPI) report weighed on broader market sentiment. The data raised concerns about the Federal Reserve’s ongoing fight against inflation and raised expectations that the central bank will maintain current interest rates.

In major after market action, Reddit (RDDT) stock declined 13.3% yesterday following disappointing user growth figures. Also, The Trade Desk (TTD) stock dropped 27.1% after fourth-quarter revenue and outlook fell short of expectations.

Looking ahead, investors will closely monitor the release of January’s Producer Price Index (PPI) on Thursday. Currently, economists anticipate headline inflation to have grown 0.3% month-over-month. This data, along with Initial Jobless Claims report for the week ended February 8, will provide guide investor expectations for future interest rate moves.

Moreover, several companies, including Airbnb (ABNB), Coinbase (COIN), Roku (ROKU), DraftKings (DKNG), Twilio (TWLO), and Palo Alto Networks (PANW), are scheduled to report earnings today.

Meanwhile, the U.S. 10-year treasury yield was down, floating near 4.605% as of writing. Simultaneously, WTI crude oil futures are trending lower, hovering near $70.71 per barrel as of the last check.

Elsewhere, European indices opened higher on Thursday as investors digested several key earnings and economic reports. Also, US-led efforts to resolve the war in Ukraine helped improve market sentiment.

Asia-Pacific Markets Traded Mixed on Thursday

Asia-Pacific indices were mixed today. Japanese stocks gained ground, buoyed by a weaker yen that boosted optimism among the country’s export-oriented industries. On the other hand, Chinese stocks declined amid concerns over Trump’s plans to impose reciprocal tariffs on countries that impose duties on U.S. imports.

Notably, the Hang Seng Index closed lower by 0.2%. Further, China’s Shanghai Composite and Shenzhen Component indices declined 0.42% and 0.77%, respectively. However, Japan’s Nikkei and Topix indices were up 1.28% and 1.18%, respectively.

Interested in more economic insights? Tune in to our LIVE webinar.

Disclosure
data.author: Radhika Saraogi

data.title: Is the Unstoppable AppLovin (APP) Stock the New AI Superstar?
data.description:
U.S.-based mobile tech company AppLovin (APP) has skyrocketed more than 700% in the past 12 months, driven by its AI-powered advertising platform. The company continued its stock market momentum with a 30% surge in after-hours trading on Wednesday, following strong Q4 results. The stock is now on the radar of both investors and analysts, even if it hasn’t made its way into their portfolios yet. With positive shifts in advertiser spending and management’s optimistic outlook, AppLovin is poised for continued growth.

Maximize Your Portfolio with Data Driven Insights:

  • Leverage the power of TipRanks' Smart Score, a data-driven tool to help you uncover top performing stocks and make informed investment decisions.
  • Monitor your stock picks and compare them to top Wall Street Analysts' recommendations with Your Smart Portfolio

In simple terms, AppLovin offers several AI-powered products that help companies extend the reach of their advertising.

AppLovin Gears Up for Next Phase of Advertising Growth

During its earnings call, AppLovin revealed it had signed an exclusive term sheet to sell its Apps business, a strategic move driven by the fact that advertising now generates most of the company’s revenues. This highlights its transformation into a dominant player in the ad-tech space.

Notably, the company’s Advertising segment brought in almost $1 billion in revenue in Q4, marking a 73% year-over-year growth.

Overall, analysts are also bullish on AppLovin’s growth prospects, citing its innovative use of AI to drive more targeted advertising, particularly within gaming apps. Additionally, Jefferies previously stated that analysis of e-commerce advertising trends reveals a significant amount of ad spending through AppLovin, indicating a strong potential for revenue growth in the near future.

AppLovin Issues Optimistic Guidance

Looking ahead, AppLovin’s management is optimistic about continued growth in the advertising sector. For Q1 2025, the company expects revenue to range between $1.355 billion and $1.385 billion, surpassing the analyst estimate of $1.32 billion. Over $1 billion of this revenue is expected to come from its advertising segment.

As AI continues to shape the advertising landscape, AppLovin’s innovative platform positions it for dominance in the industry, potentially offering growth opportunities for investors. The company’s guidance reinforces this optimistic outlook, further solidifying its growth story.

Is AppLovin a Strong Buy?

According to TipRanks, APP stock has received a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 14 Buys and three Holds assigned in the last three months. The average price target for AppLovin is $408.41, suggesting an upside of nearly 7.4% from its current price.

See more APP analyst ratings

Disclosure
data.author: Kirti Tak
